intro.text1: These Terms of Service apply to all offerings by

1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
1.text: By accessing or using the TechByP website and services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use our services.

2.title: 2. Use of Services
2.text: You may use our website and services only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree not to:
list: Use the services in any way that violates applicable laws
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems
Interfere with the proper working of our services
Use any automated means to access our services without permission

3.title: 3. Intellectual Property
3.text: All content on our website, including text, graphics, logos, and images, is the property of TechByP and is protected by intellectual property laws. You may not use our content without our express written permission.

4.title: 4. Product Information
4.text: We strive to provide accurate product information, but we do not warrant that product descriptions or other content is complete, reliable, or error-free. Specifications are subject to change without notice.

5.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
5.text: TechByP shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages resulting from the use or inability to use our services, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.

6.title: 6. Governing Law
6.text: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Germany, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

7.title: 7. Changes to Terms
7.text: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Your continued use of our services after such modifications constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms.

cookie.message: No biscuits here, just digital cookies. They help us improve your ride through our website. Carry on, and you’re giving them a thumbs-up.

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
introduction.text: TechByP ("we", "our", or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website.

dataCollection.title: 2. Data Collection
dataCollection.text: We may collect personal information such as your name, email address, phone number, and company details when you:
items: Request a quote or product information

useOfData.title: 3. Use of Data
useOfData.text: We use the information we collect to:
items: Respond to your inquiries and requests
Send periodic emails (you can unsubscribe at any time)

dataProtection.title: 4. Data Protection
dataProtection.text: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorized access, alteration, or destruction.

thirdParty.title: 5. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.text: We may employ third-party companies to facilitate our services (e.g., website hosting, analytics). These third parties have access to your information only to perform specific tasks on our behalf.

cookies.title: 6. Cookies & Tracking
cookies.text: We use cookies to enhance your experience and analyze traffic. This section explains which cookies we use and how you can manage them.
items: Necessary Cookies: Required for basic website functionality.
Analytics Cookies: We use Google Analytics to collect anonymous usage statistics and improve our offerings.
cookies.manageText: You can accept cookies to improve your experience or decline them via your browser settings. Some features may not be available if you decline cookies.

rights.title: 7. Your Rights
items: Access, update, or delete your information
Object to our processing of your data
rights.contact: To exercise these rights, please contact us at privacy@techbyp.com.

changes.title: 8. Changes to This Policy
changes.text: We may update our Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any changes by posting the new policy on this page.

home.description: What we do is rather straightforward, really. We build things properly — carefully engineered, thoughtfully designed, and tailored to suit your needs. And then, we make them even better, thanks to what you tell us. It's quality, not just from the start, but all the way through.

categoryDescriptions.bestseller: These are the machines that practically queue up to be bought—solid, dependable, and popular for good reason.
categoryDescriptions.all: Everything we've got, from the big and burly to the small and satisfyingly clever.
categoryDescriptions.smartsystems: The brainy side of soil sampling—automatic, precise, and remarkably well-mannered.
categoryDescriptions.accessory: The bits that hold everything together—quiet achievers of the support world.
categoryDescriptions.manual: Reliable, tactile tools you can trust—satisfyingly hands-on and built to last.

certifications.title: Quality Certifications

about.introText: Every machine we build is shaped by real-world feedback from the people who use them. If something can be made stronger, smarter, or just a little easier to handle, we’re on it. That’s how we work—hands-on, detail-obsessed, and always listening.
about.ourMissionText: To provide soil sampling tools that earn the trust of agricultural experts and researchers alike—from rolling fields to white coats. Crafted with German precision and backed by support teams who actually pick up the phone, we’re dedicated to making your job easier, more accurate, and a little less complicated.

milestones.event: Bodenprobetechnik Nietfeld is born — founded by Walter Nietfeld and ready to dig in.
Walter Peters takes the wheel and renames the company Bodenprobetechnik Peters GmbH. Same mission, fresh name.
TechbyP is launched — our international customers already knew us, now we made it official.
A shiny new factory rises in Quakenbrück, Germany. Precision now has a new home.
Our global distribution network gets a serious upgrade. More countries, more samples, more soil love.

footer.tagline: German-engineered gear that digs deep – literally. For soil pros who know their dirt.
footer.rights: © {{year}} TechByP – Bodenprobetechnik Peters. All rights reserved.

title: Contact TechByP

Got questions about our clever gadgets for environmental or geotechnical adventures? Our team's ready and waiting to talk shop, solve puzzles, or just chat about how to make your next soil sample a masterpiece.

shipping.question: What are your shipping options?
shipping.answer: We ship worldwide via DHL, DPD, and other trusted carriers. Standard shipping within Germany takes 2-3 business days. International shipping times vary by destination. Expedited shipping options are available.

training.question: Do you offer training for your equipment?
training.answer: Yes, we provide comprehensive training programs for all our soil sampling equipment. Training can be conducted at your location or at our facility in Quakenbrück. Contact our sales team to arrange training sessions.

distributor.question: How can I become a distributor?
distributor.answer: We're always interested in partnering with qualified distributors worldwide. Please contact our sales department with details about your company and market experience to begin the application process.

mp1.description: Intact 0–90 cm soil cores, scientific precision, German stubbornness.
mp1.herodescription: It's not flashy. It doesn't shout. It doesn't do Wi-Fi. But what it does do—better than almost anything else—is give you a perfect, uninterrupted soil core, from surface to 90 centimetres below. Yes, the whole story, from crust to subsoil, all in one go. It's like an MRI for the earth.
mp1.detailedDescription:
Universities love this thing. And not just because it looks vaguely like a rocket launcher. It gives full visibility of the soil profile—no mixing, no layering guesswork, just a clean, vertical slice of the Earth's innards. Perfect for N-min sampling, yes, but also for those who like to actually see what's going on down there.

Now, it's hydraulically powered, semi-automatic, and very clever. It'll dig with millimetric politeness, and if it hits something unfriendly—like a rock or an old Roman coin—it simply stops. No drama. No broken bits.

And best of all: it's German engineering distilled to the essentials. Clean, efficient, utterly logical. Like Beethoven… if he were a soil sampler.
features: Full-length (0–90 cm) intact soil core for profile visibility
Centimetre-precise depth control
Obstacle detection with automatic stop
Manual core removal for sample integrity
Fits pickups, trailers, and lightweight vehicles
Upgradeable to MP-3.90 or MP-4.100
howToUse: Step 1 – Impact Insertion: A grooved rod is driven into the soil using a high-frequency hydraulic hammer. It sounds aggressive, but it's all very civilised.
Step 2 – Hydraulic Retraction with Rotation: The rod is rotated and retracted hydraulically, ensuring the core stays pristine. Surgical precision, minus the scalpel.
Step 3 – Core Revelation: The unbroken soil core is exposed for scientific admiration. All layers visible—from humus to hardpan.
Step 4 – Manual Core Discharge: The core is manually removed. It's a quiet, satisfying moment of science. Like pulling data out of the Earth with your hands.

technicalSpecs.Mounting: Pickup trucks, trailers, light agricultural vehicles

testimonials.quote: The MP-1.90 is a true workhorse. It gives us full core visibility, which is essential for our teaching and fieldwork programs.
testimonials.author: Prof. Klaus Brenner
testimonials.company: University of Göttingen, Dept. of Soil Science

testimonials.quote: It's beautifully overengineered, in a very German way. Every core we extract is pristine. Students love using it.
testimonials.author: Dr. Lucia Romero
testimonials.company: University of Zaragoza – Faculty of Agronomy

mp2.description: Automatic nitrogen profiling without the faff.
mp2.herodescription: Say hello to the MP-2.60 — a wonderfully practical piece of machinery for N-min soil sampling. It goes down to 60 cm, gets what it needs, and carries on without asking for tea or praise. Designed to be reliable, not ridiculous, it's ideal for anyone who likes things to work properly the first time.
mp2.detailedDescription:
In a world of overcomplication, the MP-2.60 is refreshingly straightforward. It's built specifically for automatic nitrogen profiling—none of that faffing about with manual augers or guesswork. It uses a high-frequency hydraulic hammer to punch through soil like a gentle jackhammer, then quietly rotates and lifts the rod before politely dropping the sample into its container.

It has smart internal logic to decide which bits of soil to keep, and if you fancy it, you can even enable layer separation for horizon-specific sampling. The samples themselves land in waterproof containers, and the whole unit mounts comfortably on trailers, pickups, or basically anything that rolls and doesn't fall apart.

And should your research ambitions outgrow its 60 cm limit, don't panic — the MP-2.60 is fully upgradeable to the MP-3.90 or MP-4.100.
features: Fully automatic extraction to 60 cm
Smart logic: discards or keeps samples based on your settings
Impact-driven sampling with auto-discharge
Layer separation option for horizon-specific sampling
Waterproof sample containers
Customisable sampling depth and horizon count
Obstacle detection and safe auto-shutdown
Built for real, daily use – not just weekend hobbyists
howToUse: Drives a grooved rod into the soil using a high-frequency hydraulic hammer — yes, it sounds as satisfying as it is
Hydraulically rotates and retracts the rod without disturbing your samples
Samples are automatically discharged into their proper containers
Optional layer separation sorts the soil by horizon during extraction
applications: Field sampling on a budget with ambitions

technicalSpecs.Mounting Options: Pickup, trailer, or anything vaguely roadworthy
technicalSpecs.Special Features: Obstacle detection, Waterproof containers, Digital keyboard

testimonials.quote: Reliable, easy to use, and delivers exactly what we need for our soil nitrate work.
testimonials.author: Prof. Lars Becker

testimonials.quote: A great choice for any team starting out with automated sampling. Solid performance, low maintenance.
testimonials.author: Dr. Hannah Lee

mp3.description: Tough enough to handle the soil, smart enough to work efficiently.
mp3.herodescription: The MP-3.90 is what happens when you take German thinking, sprinkle in some overengineering, and then ask it to dig holes for science. Designed to extract soil samples down to 95 cm without so much as a grunt from the operator, it can do 3-layer separation, auto-discharge, and decide (all on its own) when to give up if the soil fights back.
mp3.detailedDescription:
Now, precision and efficiency aren't just buzzwords here—they're thoughtfully built in, so you can concentrate on the data rather than wrestling with stubborn dirt. It manages tough soil with barely any fuss from the operator, making it a rather dependable companion for serious scientific work or proper field research.

It'll slot neatly onto pickups, trailers, tractors, or just about anything vaguely roadworthy, with customisable depth and horizon settings to suit whatever you need. Plus, your samples end up safe and sound in waterproof containers, which is a nice touch.

And here's the kicker—it's built for proper, professional use, not some weekend warrior's toy. Solid, reliable, and quietly competent—exactly what you'd expect from German engineering, really.
features: Fully automatic extraction down to 95 cm
Optional 3-layer separation for horizon-specific sampling
Takes 3 consecutive samples without leaving the seat
Mounts on pickups, trailers, or anything vaguely roadworthy
Auto-discharge and sample sorting during extraction
Customizable sampling depth and horizon count
Waterproof containers for sample storage
Auto-shutdown when it hits a rock and says 'nope'
Smart logic: discards or keeps samples based on settings
Built for serious, continuous use — this isn't your casual, once-in-a-while gadget.
howToUse: Drives a serious-looking grooved rod into the soil with a high-frequency hydraulic hammer — think of it as a very precise poke
Hydraulically rotates and retracts the rod, avoiding the sort of brutish yanking that upsets soil and engineers alike
Automatically empties the auger on the way up — no scooping, no fiddling, just a polite drop into the sample containers
Distributes sample material neatly into separate containers depending on soil horizon — because even dirt should know where it belongs
applications: Field work where speed, accuracy, and coffee breaks matter

technicalSpecs.Special Features: Obstacle detection, Waterproof containers, Digital keyboard

testimonials.quote: Perfect for our wetland research. Works exceptionally well in clay-rich soils where other augers fail.
testimonials.author: Dr. Maria Santos

testimonials.quote: The half-cylinder design is brilliant for cohesive soils. Sample quality is consistently excellent.
testimonials.author: Robert Taylor

mp4.description: Deep soil, sorted smartly. No fuss, just depth.
mp4.herodescription: This is the MP-4.100 — imagine if a hydraulic hammer and a PhD in soil science had a child. It's a fully automatic sampler that goes down a full 100 cm, pulls out up to four layers, and sorts them like a butler with a geological hobby. It's what you'd get if you asked Germany to make a mole.
mp4.detailedDescription:
Designed for those who demand depth and discipline, the MP-4.100 gets right into the soil and does the job with no grumbling and no manual scooping. A hydraulic impact system powers a grooved rod into the ground, where it quietly gets on with the job of extracting science-grade samples. Once retracted, it automatically empties the goods into their proper places.

It's got smart logic that decides whether to keep or toss material based on your settings, and a layer separation mechanism that's surprisingly polite about where each horizon goes. Mount it on a pickup, trailer, or anything vaguely roadworthy and off you go.
features: Fully automatic extraction to 100 cm
Optional 4-layer separation for horizon-specific sampling
Auto-discharge and sample sorting during extraction
Smart logic: discards or keeps samples based on your settings
Customizable sampling depth and horizon count
Mounts on pickups, trailers, or anything vaguely roadworthy
Obstacle detection and safe auto-shutdown
Waterproof sample containers
Digital keyboard for on-the-fly adjustments
Built for real, daily use – not just occasional science days
howToUse: Drives a grooved rod into the soil using a high-frequency hydraulic hammer — elegantly brutal
Hydraulically rotates and retracts the rod — no yanking, no drama
Automatically empties the auger into appropriate containers on the way up
Distributes sample material neatly into separate containers depending on soil horizon
applications: Field work requiring serious depth and sample fidelity

technicalSpecs.Special Features: Obstacle detection, Waterproof containers, Digital keyboard

testimonials.quote: It handles heavy soils and deep profiles better than any manual method we've tried. Truly hands-off and precise.
testimonials.author: Dr. Felix Hartmann

testimonials.quote: Reliable and smart — the sample logic saves us hours of post-processing.
testimonials.author: Dr. Nina Weber

dh.description: Efficient, dependable, and quietly confident. Soil sampling, but with a bit of hydraulic muscle behind it.
dh.herodescription: The DH-1.30 is a robust, hydraulic soil sampler built to perform with quiet confidence and steady power—no overcomplication, just solid results. Drilling smoothly up to 30 centimetres, it's powered by a hydraulic engine—so you get plenty of grunt, no electrics needed. A little louder than its electric cousin, sure, but it's no show-off. Just reliable, solid work, day in, day out.
dh.detailedDescription:
The DH-1.30 takes the sensible design of its electric sibling but swaps out the battery for a hydraulic engine — ideal if you've got access to hydraulic power and want a bit more torque.

Depth control is sharp and precise, with millimetre adjustments to handle uneven ground as if it's nothing.

Operation is delightfully straightforward: one button, two preset depths — because life's too short for fiddly controls.

It weighs a sturdy 45 kg, built tough enough to handle real fieldwork but light enough that you won't need a forklift or a strongman to move it.

Easily mounted on quads, trailers, pickups, or light-duty vehicles, this thing is made for versatility.

For those who prefer the classic reliability of hydraulic power with dependable German engineering, this sampler is the sensible choice.
features: Hydraulic drive for dependable, powerful drilling
Precision millimetre depth adjustment
Not affected by vehicle suspension—no surprises underground
Two preset depths for fast switching
Compact and portable at 45 kg
Mounts easily on a range of light vehicles
applications: Precision farming (with a bit of old-school power)
howToUse: Park your rig and position the sampler where the soil's calling.
Select your preferred depth (yes, down to the millimetre).
Hit the button and let the hydraulic muscle do the heavy lifting.
Pull out your perfect soil core and feel quietly pleased with yourself.

technicalSpecs.Mounting Options: Quad, pickup, trailer, or light-duty vehicle

testimonials.quote: The DH-1.30's hydraulic drive means no compromises on power or precision. Perfect for tougher soils and longer field days.
testimonials.author: Sven Becker

testimonials.quote: Mounted on our trailer, the DH-1.30 handles everything with quiet confidence. Depth control is spot on every time.
testimonials.author: Maria Schneider

testimonials.quote: A rugged, reliable machine with enough grunt to make fieldwork feel less like hard work.
testimonials.author: Tomás Herrera

de.description: Smart, silent, and very nimble. Making soil sampling look almost effortless.
de.herodescription: The DE-1.30 is the sensible, compact soil sampler that quietly gets on with the job—drilling down up to 30 centimetres without making a fuss or needing you to chain-smoke while it works. It's electric, so no fumes, no noise, just clean, reliable soil sampling.
de.detailedDescription:
Building on the trusty N2012 model, the DE-1.30 uses an electric drill that's so precise it could probably thread a needle—if soil sampling were into embroidery. Millimetre adjustments mean you get exactly the depth you want, no matter how bumpy or unpredictable the terrain. It's a bit like a polite butler who won't let you down.

The control unit is simplicity itself—press a button and off it goes. Two preset depths let you switch between sampling jobs faster than you can say 'groundbreaking technology' (see what I did there?). No faffing about with complicated menus or cryptic controls.

At just 45 kilograms and powered by a 24 V battery pack (sold separately), it's light enough to be nimble but tough enough to handle real-world fieldwork. No need to call in a crane or an ex-army sergeant to move it.

The DE-1.30 can be easily mounted on quads, trailers, and a variety of light vehicles, making it versatile for many field conditions.

So, if you want precise, dependable soil samples without the petrol engine drama or the noise of a heavy drill, the DE-1.30 is your go-to machine. It's German engineering with a quiet, electric twist that just gets the job done.
features: Fully electric drilling for clean, precise sampling
Millimeter-precise depth adjustment (because close enough isn't good enough)
Independent of vehicle suspension to avoid depth tantrums
Two quick-select depth presets
Compact and lightweight at 45 kg
Runs on a 24 V external battery pack (sold separately)
applications: Precision farming with a bit of style
howToUse: Position the sampler where the soil needs a look-see.
Choose your depth. Millimeter increments, because we're picky.
Press the button and watch the electric drill politely do its job.
Pull out the soil core and marvel at your perfectly sampled earth.

technicalSpecs.Mounting Options: Quads, pickup, trailer, or light-duty vehicle

testimonials.quote: The DE-1.30's precision and ease of use have transformed our soil sampling routine. The electric drill is whisper-quiet and remarkably reliable.
testimonials.author: Sven Becker

testimonials.quote: Mounting the DE-1.30 on our quad made fieldwork incredibly efficient. Its depth control and battery power are perfect for on-the-go sampling.
testimonials.author: Maria Schneider

testimonials.quote: Lightweight but robust, the DE-1.30 handles tough soil conditions with ease. The user-friendly control unit is a big plus in remote locations.
testimonials.author: Tomás Herrera

boprob.description: Tow it, sample it, then keep on moving—no fuss, no waiting.
boprob.herodescription: This is the BOPROB III—also known, rather jauntily, as the The Sixteen Express. It's a trailer that takes soil samples while you drive. Yes, really. No stopping, no button-mashing, no shouting at interns. Just tow it behind something with wheels and let it get on with the job.
boprob.detailedDescription:
Now, the The Sixteen Express is what you'd call a clever bit of kit. It's a tow-behind soil sampler designed to do its work while you keep moving. Which, frankly, is how everything should work—but doesn't.

Sampling happens at a rather brisk pace thanks to a hydraulic hammer and a rotating crank that goes the full 360°, stabbing into the ground like a polite little jackhammer, then politely ejecting the core into the sample magazine.

Everything is watched over by a 7-inch touchscreen—lovely bit of kit—so you know exactly what it's doing at any time. There's even an optional camera, in case you want to see it work, or just admire your purchase.

The wheels are large and soft—235/60 R16, if you're into that sort of thing—which means minimal ground pressure and no getting bogged down in a muddy field, unless you've really tried.

It hitches easily to tractors, pickups, quads—or anything else with a tow hitch, really. No fiddling, no adapters, no workshop heroics required. And once you're done, it unhitches just as easily.

If you're driving too fast—or too slowly, perhaps because you're admiring the scenery—the system simply refuses to sample. Which is brilliant, because it means the machine is smarter than the average driver.
features: Hydraulic impact and rotation system
360° rotating crank with auto-discharge
Samples while driving—no need to stop
7-inch touchscreen display for control and monitoring
Optional onboard camera for visual feedback
Low soil compaction from 235/60 R16 wheels
No tractor mods needed—trailer mounts quickly
Speed-sensitive protection against misuse
applications: Precision farming with minimal fuel use
Soil health monitoring on large acreages
howToUse: Attach the trailer to a suitable tractor or tow-capable vehicle—no modifications needed.
Set your desired depth and sample interval on the touchscreen.
Drive at 4–15 km/h and let the machine sample automatically.
Check camera and screen as needed, or just carry on farming.
When 16 samples are collected, empty and reload the magazine.

technicalSpecs.Mounting Options: Tow-behind trailer (fits tractors or any vehicle with standard hitch)

testimonials.quote: We finished a 10-hectare survey in under an hour. The The Sixteen Express just works. No drama, no stopping.
testimonials.author: Lena Hoffmann

testimonials.quote: The touchscreen is responsive, and the sampling quality is spot on. It handles our clay-heavy soils beautifully.
testimonials.author: Henrik Voss

testimonials.quote: It saves wear on the tractor and the driver. One of the best investments for large-scale sampling we've made.
testimonials.author: Jürgen Möller

laydown.name: Lay-down Frame
specs: Standard 50 mm tow hitch with a 13‑pin 12 V socket required.
EN 10025 S355 steel, powder-coated, 4 mm thick
laydown.description: A no‑nonsense mounting bracket for your sampler—fits the back end of most civilized vehicles without turning them into Franken-trucks.
laydown.herodescription: Brilliantly sensible. This lay-down frame, TÜV-certified Mount Kit bolts to your tow hitch like it belongs there. Supports our MP, DH, and DE series samplers.
laydown.detailedDescription:
Mounted on the truck bed connected to that place where your tow bar lives—just below the boot lid and above the exhaust pipe drama—this frame lets you carry a full soil sampler with elegance. Fold it down to operate, fold it up to drive off to dinner. It's sturdy, tested, and painted like a German tool cabinet.

Clients can choose their level of involvement: some prefer to install the frame themselves with the help of our foolproof instructions; others drop by with their vehicle and let us handle the mounting and hydraulic wizardry; and for those who enjoy instant gratification, we also offer fully kitted-out field rigs—pre-mounted, tested, and generally quite smug about it.
features: Tow‑hitch + 13‑pin mounting (no welding, no trauma)
TÜV‑approved for road and field use
Structural EN S355 steel, powder-coated
Quick‑release M16 mounting hardware included
Foldable design tested to 10 000 deployment cycles
Supports external power packs (hydraulic or battery)
applications: Hydraulic MP‑series or electric DE/DH samplers
Seasonal use or fully mounted field rigs
howToUse: Vehicle has ISO 50 mm tow ball? Check.
Vehicle has a 13‑pin 12 V socket (for DE‑1.30)? Check.
Hydraulic output available (135–220 bar @60 L/min)?
Fold down, test power, and begin sampling.

technicalSpecs.Material: EN S355 steel, 4 mm thick, RAL 9005 powder-coat
technicalSpecs.Mount Interface: 50 mm ISO tow hitch + DE‑1.30 13‑pin plug
technicalSpecs.Hydraulic Input: 135–220 bar; 20–90 L/min; DN 19/¾″ hose, burst ≥500 bar
technicalSpecs.Power Options: External hydraulic power pack or battery
technicalSpecs.Included Hardware: M16×45‑8.8 + self‑locking M10 bolts, all plated
technicalSpecs.Bracket Compatibility: All MP (MP‑1.90 to MP‑4.100), DH, and DE models

tph.name: Three‑Point Hitch
specs: Fits Cat‑2 or Cat‑3 tractor 3‑point PTO systems
Compatible with all MP-series, DH, and DE soil sampling machines
tph.description: Makes your tractor be a soil-sampling beast, harnessing PTO power for hydraulic sampler integration. Tough, reliable, no-nonsense.
tph.herodescription: Tractor 3‑Point Hitch Frame: The heavyweight champion for Cat‑2/3 tractors, designed to mount your hydraulic soil sampler straight onto the tractor's PTO system with a hydraulic pump platform to match.
tph.detailedDescription:
Imagine the steel backbone your tractor never knew it needed. Crafted from thick A‑frame steel and cloaked in black epoxy armor, this mount bolts cleanly onto your tractor's lift arms. It's built to survive farm life — rain, mud, and the odd tractor tantrum included. The kit comes with lift bars and all mounting bolts, so no improvising with rusty nails or duct tape.

Suitable for compact tractors with lift arm spacing around 50 to 60 inches, this frame supports the hydraulic pump directly powered by your PTO shaft, giving you efficient hydraulic flow for smooth soil sampling. It's the upgrade that makes your tractor feel like it's ready to take on the field Olympics.
features: Welded A‑frame steel—sturdy enough for actual work
PTO pump mount—because zip ties aren't engineering
Black epoxy finish—like paint, but smarter
Comes with bolts and lift bars—no scrounging required
Fits Cat‑2/3 lift arms (50–60")
Works with MP, DH, and DE samplers
howToUse: Attach to lift arms—tighten like you mean it
Mount pump baseplate—don't cross-thread
Connect PTO and route hoses neatly
Fit sampler, test, nod approvingly

technicalSpecs.Construction: Welded heavy-duty A‑frame steel with epoxy finish
technicalSpecs.PTO Compatibility: Standard tractor PTO drives hydraulic pump
technicalSpecs.Includes: Lift bars, mounting bolts, and installation hardware
technicalSpecs.Frame Size: Fits 50–60" lift arm spacing typical of compact tractors
technicalSpecs.Compatibility: Works with all MP-series, DH, and DE soil sampling machines

testimonials.quote: Bolted right on, no surprises. Used it with our MP-2.60 — solid frame, everything fit, no rattling. Would buy again.
testimonials.author: J. Bauer

testimonials.quote: Got it on the tractor in under an hour. PTO pump runs smooth, sampler mounts clean. Just works.
testimonials.author: L. Schneider

fc.name: Full Conversion
specs: Custom powder-coated steel frame with aluminium tool panels
OEM parts removed and stored; frame TÜV-certified
Supports all MP-series, DH, and DE soil samplers
fc.description: Turns your utility vehicle or pickup into a no-nonsense soil sampling beast. Bed off, frame on, tools loaded.
fc.herodescription: Full Conversion Kit replaces the standard truck bed or UTV platform with a custom steel frame and aluminium covers—purpose-built for soil sampling with MP-series, DH, and DE machines. TÜV-approved, road-legal, and rather clever.
fc.detailedDescription:
If your UTV or pickup dreams of greatness, this is its moment. We carefully remove the stock bed and replace them with a steel conversion frame, wrapped in aluminium panels that scream 'professional operator' louder than a flat-out Hilux on gravel.

It gives you proper mounting for all our machines, integrated storage for coolers, tools, lighting—basically everything but the packed lunch. Certified by TÜV, built for serious campaigns, and oddly satisfying to look at.
features: Universal fit for pickups and UTVs (Hilux, Gator, Kubota, etc.)
Aluminium-covered steel frame: durable and corrosion-resistant
Supports tool storage, lighting, coolers, and sampler systems
TÜV-certified and road-legal in EU
Fully compatible with all MP, DE, and DH machines
applications: Fleet builds for large-scale soil sampling
Upgrades for agricultural and environmental research vehicles
howToUse: Select your sampling machine — MP-series, DH, or DE. Sensible choices, all of them.
Ensure your vehicle has a hitch receiver or trailer coupling. If not, one can be arranged. We're not savages.
Bring us the vehicle — or let us source a suitable model.
We fit the steel frame, bolt on the sampler, handle the paperwork, and send you off to do battle with soil.

technicalSpecs.Compatible Vehicles: Pickups (Hilux, Ranger, etc.) and UTVs (Gator,RTV, Ranger, etc.)
technicalSpecs.Vehicle Requirements: Standard hitch receiver or trailer coupling. Of course.

testimonials.quote: Dropped off the Hilux, picked it up a week later ready to sample. Clean build, everything fit right. No hassle.
testimonials.author: T. Klein

testimonials.quote: Solid frame, smart layout. We run two DH units off converted Rangers — way easier than trailers in tight fields.
testimonials.author: M. Becker

trailer.name: Special Trailers
specs: Hot-dip galvanised—rust doesn't stand a chance.
Braked or non-braked options: choose your level of control
Designed to fit MP-series, DH, and DE samplers perfectly
trailer.description: These trailers transport MP-series, DH/DE systems effortlessly across anything that isn't actually a wall.
trailer.herodescription:
Special Trailers – Field Haulers are rugged, hot-dip galvanised trailers with both braked and non-braked options, designed to carry your soil sampling kit—be it MP, DH, or DE—through fields and farms with Germanic precision.
They come with clever storage for tools, lights, and coolers—so you can work all day without the usual fuss.
trailer.detailedDescription:
With a fixed 1.35 m track width and jack-castor wheels, the Field Hauler maneuvers like a sensible shopping trolley—only with more steel and fewer weak points.

Choose braked models for heavier loads or steeper inclines—up to 3,500 kg GTW—so you can haul soil samplers without dreaming of new brake pads every trip.
features: Hot-dip galvanised body and chassis—long life, fewer complaints
Braked and non-braked options—pick your handling style
Single-axle with mechanical drum brakes for stable towing
Shock absorbers for 100 km/h road approval—smooth ride, no rattling
Track width 1.35 m with jack‑castor wheels included
LT285/75 R16 aluminium rims or MT215/75 R15 steel—tread the fields, not flip over
Support wheel included for easy hand maneuvering
Built to haul MP-series, DH, and DE samplers—no IKEA instructions required
Integrated tool, light, and cooler storage—work smarter, not harder
applications: Field transport of MP, DH, and DE sampler kits
Hauling crates, coolboxes, and more across farms
Light-duty environments, both rural and contracting sites
howToUse: Select braked or non-braked depending on your towing vehicle and load.
Hitched with standard 50 mm ball coupling—no exotic engineering required.
Load your sampler and kit, secure all straps and tie-downs.
Tow it at sensible speeds—no drifting or unintended adventure.

technicalSpecs.Load Options: 550 kg (braked/non-braked), heavy‑duty upgrade to 1,500 kg GTW
technicalSpecs.Rims/Tyre Size: LT285/75 R16 on aluminium rims or MT215/75 R15
technicalSpecs.Brakes: Mechanical drum brakes (braked version), single-axle configuration

testimonials.quote: A rugged, reliable machine with enough grunt to make fieldwork feel less like hard work.
testimonials.author: Tomás Herrera

powerpack.name: Power Pack
specs: Hydraulic or electric powered to fit your power needs
Portable, durable steel casing built for rough fieldwork
LCD battery status on electric models—know your charge at a glance
Return and suction filtration included for clean hydraulic operation
powerpack.description: For those running MP or DH units without onboard power, these packs deliver consistent hydraulic or electric energy wherever your work takes you.
powerpack.herodescription:
Power Packs are like the dependable butlers of soil sampling—offering reliable, portable power so your machines don't throw a tantrum. Whether you fancy the classic hydraulic grunt or prefer the whisper-quiet electric charm, these packs have your back.
Wrapped in a rugged steel casing tougher than your average tractor, with sensible perks like LCD battery status and filtration systems, they're the trusty sidekick you never knew you needed out in the field.
powerpack.detailedDescription:
The hydraulic model, perfectly matched to MP-series and DH-series machines, boasts a 13 HP gasoline engine and a 22 L oil tank, pushing out 135 bar pressure at 20 L/min—that's serious grunt for when you want to feel like a proper mechanical overlord.

The electric cousin, designed specifically for the DE-series, sports a 24 V / 100 Ah battery, a stainless steel suit of armour, and an LCD screen to keep an eye on its own power levels. Ideal for those who prefer a quieter, cleaner, and frankly more polite approach to soil sampling.

Both models are portable, ruggedly built, and come with return/suction filtration to keep your precious hydraulics from choking on dirt—because nobody likes premature wear, do they?
features: Hydraulic or electric powered to suit your workflow
Rugged steel casing with corrosion protection
LCD battery status on electric models
Return and suction filtration for hydraulic longevity
Portable and easy to mount or transport
applications: Power source for MP-series and DH-series soil samplers
Field operations requiring independent hydraulic or electric power
Remote sampling locations without vehicle power supply
howToUse: Pick hydraulic or electric depending on how much oomph your sampler demands (and how fancy you feel).
Bolt it on securely to your vehicle or trailer—no improvising with duct tape, please.
Keep an eye on power levels via LCD (electric) or fuel/oil gauges (hydraulic)—because surprises are for birthday parties, not fieldwork.
Top up oil and change filters regularly—treat it well, and it'll keep your sampler humming like a charm.

technicalSpecs.Hydraulic Model: 13 HP gasoline engine, 22 L oil tank, 135 bar pressure, 20 L/min flow, 12 V electrical system
technicalSpecs.Electric Model: 24 V / 100 Ah battery, LCD status display, stainless steel enclosure

testimonials.quote: A rugged, reliable machine with enough grunt to make fieldwork feel less like hard work.
testimonials.author: Tomás Herrera

spareparts.name: Recommended Spare Parts
specs: Critical components for high-wear and mission-critical assemblies
Individually packaged, labelled, and field-service ready
spareparts.description: Because nothing ruins a good day in the field like a broken fitting or a leaky seal. These are the official, recommended spare parts—hand-picked for your MP, DH, DE or BOPROB III machine, and ready to get you out of trouble before it starts.
spareparts.herodescription:
Think of these as the first-aid kit for your soil sampler. Whether you're out in a bog in Bavaria or a dust bowl in Bulgaria, having the right OEM spare parts on hand turns a potential disaster into a smugly handled hiccup.
Each kit includes the usual suspects—O-rings, hydraulic couplings, filters, belts, and the bits that quietly work the hardest. No improvising with baling wire or borrowed parts from your mate's combine.
spareparts.detailedDescription:
These are the recommended spare parts kits for the MP-series, DH-series, DE-series, and BOPROB III samplers. And no, they're not optional. They're what the engineers use. What the service techs use. What you should have in the toolbox if you're even slightly serious about uptime.

Inside each kit, you'll find the usual suspects—seals, filters, hydraulic fittings, relays, and pressure lines—the bits that keep the fluid where it's supposed to be and the current flowing in the right direction. It's like carrying a spare pair of underpants on a long-haul flight: you hope you won't need them, but you'll be damn glad if you do.

Parts are individually packaged and labelled, so you don't spend half a day figuring out if that washer fits the return line or the oil filter. And of course, they're all factory-certified and designed to slot right in without any butchery.
features: Recommended spare parts for MP, DH, DE and BOPROB III soil samplers
Includes critical wear components and emergency repair items
Factory-approved for seamless compatibility
Clearly labelled and packaged for field use
Helps reduce downtime during critical sampling campaigns
applications: Field repairs and preventive maintenance on BPT soil samplers
Keeping high-wear components in service-ready condition
Avoiding project delays due to unavailable parts
howToUse: Keep one kit per machine—because nobody likes cannibalising parts from the unit you're not using.
Replace high-wear components according to service intervals or visible wear.
Use only the labelled components for their intended locations (guessing is not heroic, it's expensive).
Restock your kit after use so you're never caught without it twice.

technicalSpecs.Compatibility: MP-1.90, MP-2.60, MP-3.90, MP-4.100, MP-1.120, DH-series, DE-1.30, BOPROB III
technicalSpecs.Included Parts: Hydraulic couplings, filters, O-rings, solenoids, belts, relays, pressure lines (varies by kit)

testimonials.quote: I used to wing it with random parts—until I spent two hours in the mud rebuilding a valve with chewing gum. Never again.
testimonials.author: Elena Mihăilescu

coolbox.name: Coolbox 95L
specs: Digital display, USB charging, and app control
Works on 12/24 V DC or 230 V AC power
coolbox.description: Because sometimes your samples—or your beer—deserve better than lukewarm misery. With this 95L beast, you're bringing a portable arctic tundra to the job site. Possibly overkill. But definitely brilliant.
coolbox.herodescription:
You know the feeling—you're halfway up a mountain, everything's on schedule, and then someone opens the cooler to find... soup. Not anymore. The Chiller turns smug grins into icy relief, literally.
It cools. It freezes. It glows. And yes, it has an app—because your phone isn't smug enough already. German engineers might call this a Kühlbox. We call it 'bringing the fridge, because we can.'
coolbox.detailedDescription:
The Coolbox 95L is, frankly, more capable than most hotel minibars. Dual compartments, each configurable as fridge or freezer, mean you can keep your sample kit on one side and lunch on the other—and still have room for smug satisfaction.

It's powered by a high-efficiency LG compressor (which is more than your car's AC can say), and chills down to -20°C even when it's hot enough to fry an egg on the roof rack. Use it via the control panel or the Alpicool app if you're the sort of person who controls their fridge from a hammock.

For extended autonomy, pair it with the optional battery (not included), giving you 4 to 24 hours of standalone cooling. Or plug it into the vehicle and never worry again. It's built to take a beating—though we advise against that, obviously.
features: Dual compartments can cool or freeze independently
Digital display with touch controls and mobile app
Built-in LED lighting and USB charging port
Lids open from both sides for maximum faffing potential
Runs on 12/24 V DC or 110–240 V AC
applications: Transporting temperature-sensitive samples in hot climates
Multi-day expeditions without sacrificing cold beverages
Keeping gel packs and specimens within lab-compliant temperatures
Emergency beer preservation at remote job sites
howToUse: Plug into vehicle or AC power before loading
Set target temperatures via the control panel or app
Use compartments as fridge-freezer, fridge-fridge, or freezer-freezer
Load samples upright, and avoid overstuffing (yes, we know you will anyway)
Clean regularly unless you're conducting a microbial growth study

led.name: LED Work Light 1700
specs: Pivot-mounted LED work light for near-field illumination
led.description: Darkness is no excuse. With 1700 lumens packed into a compact, armored unit, this LED work light is what happens when you cross a headlamp with a small sun. Whether you're mid-sample or mid-swear word, you'll see everything—clearly.
led.herodescription:
Sampling in the dark? Forgot the sun? No problem. The Midnight Sun delivers seriously powerful illumination without the heat, the noise, or the diesel bill.
Mount it, point it, fire it up—then wonder how you ever lived without it. It's not romantic lighting. It's get-the-job-done lighting.
led.detailedDescription:
The LED Work Light 1700 is a field-tested, no-nonsense luminaire designed to cut through darkness like a chainsaw through butter. With a brutal 1700 lumen output, it delivers focused near-field lighting for sampling rigs, machinery, or any situation where 'I think I see something' isn't good enough.

Built with a black anodized aluminum housing and meeting IP6K7 and IP6K9K standards, this unit shrugs off water jets, dust storms, and probably most of your mistakes. A pivoting bracket allows precise aiming, while the patterned lens keeps the beam wide and usable.

Compatible with 12–24 V DC systems, the light draws 24 W and comes with open cable ends for easy integration. Assembly by qualified personnel is required—because melting wires is less funny in real life.
features: 1700 lumen output from 4 high-intensity LEDs
Pivoting bracket for precise aiming
IP6K9K and IP6K7 ingress protection
Patterned lens for near-field spread
Compact and rugged aluminum housing
applications: Equipment lighting on rigs and trailers
Work zones in tunnels, forests, or 'middle of nowhere'
howToUse: Mount securely using included pivot bracket
Wire into 12 or 24 V DC supply (assembly by qualified personnel)
Aim beam toward work zone
Avoid staring directly into it unless you enjoy retinal regret

camera.name: External Camera
specs: Mounts directly to your machine frame
Live video feed to your mobile or tablet via app
75° field of view with IR night vision up to 15 m
camera.description: Why guess what's going on behind the rig when you can watch it happen in real time. The Watchtower mounts to your machine and streams live footage straight to your cab. No cables. No blind spots. No surprises.
camera.herodescription:
Ever tried reversing your rig blind with a 3-ton sampler on the back? Not ideal. The Watchtower gives you eyes on the drill—from the comfort of your driver's seat. Monitor every move, strike, and wobble.
Connect via 4G. View from your phone. Because being in two places at once is now technically possible.
camera.detailedDescription:
The Watchtower is a field-ready 5MP camera built for mounting directly to your soil sampling machine. Once installed, it streams live video to your tablet or phone using 4G—so you can see what's happening at the back of the rig without leaving your seat (or shouting across a muddy field).

Its 75° viewing angle gives full visibility of the sampling head, while infrared night vision extends that confidence well into the dusk. Whether you're checking alignment, confirming core drop, or just admiring your drilling skills, it's all visible in real time.

The app is free, the setup takes minutes, and there's no need for local Wi-Fi. With an optional micro SD card (up to 128 GB), you can even record operations for documentation or training. Setup is plug-and-play, with one important caveat: insert the SIM card properly or spend the afternoon diagnosing your own impatience.

It's rainproof, mud-proof, and stubbornly reliable—exactly what you want from something bolted to a vibrating machine in the middle of nowhere.
features: Live video streaming over 4G network
75° fixed-angle lens with night vision
Mounts directly to TechByp sampling machines
App control with image adjustment (WDR, BLC, etc.)
Optional onboard recording via micro SD
applications: Monitoring soil sampling operations from inside the vehicle
Avoiding blind drilling and unexpected surprises
howToUse: Mount securely to machine using bracket
Insert SIM card and optional SD card
Connect to 12V power supply on the rig
Launch app to stream live feed from camera
Wipe clean occasionally—but only when unplugged!

probes.name: Probes, Dipsticks and Accessories
specs: Conical taper profile (40→38 mm / 35→33 mm / 30→28 mm)
Compatible with N-min sampling protocols (VDLUFA, ISO 14255)
probes.description: The gold standard for manual N-min sampling—precision-engineered, tapered drill rods built for hard soils and lab-grade accuracy.
probes.herodescription: Not just any rods—these are razor-sharp, tool steel scalpels for the soil aficionado who demands precision. Their tapered design means less fuss, less friction, and those laser-etched 10 cm marks? Perfect for knowing exactly how deep you've poked—because soil layers don't like to be disturbed, and neither should you.
probes.detailedDescription:
Designed for those who take soil seriously (and themselves, a little less): three slick tapered sizes—40→38 mm for 0-30 cm, 35→33 mm for 30-60 cm, and 30→28 mm for 60-90 cm—cut through stubborn clay and gravel like a hot knife through butter, thanks to pre-broken edges ready to do the hard work for you.

Each rod sports laser-etched 10 cm increments that won't fade faster than your enthusiasm on a Monday morning. Pair them with toggles (Items 1030/1031) for easy lifting, and a chisel (Item 1051) to strip samples clean—because sampling should feel like a gentle nudge, not an arm wrestle.
features: Three tapered diameters minimize friction and maximize sampling ease
HRC 52 hardened tool steel for durability and strength
Laser-etched depth markings every 10 cm for precise sampling
Pre-broken cutting edges for consistent, clean penetration
Compatible with toggle handles and chisels for efficient sample extraction
applications: Manual sampling in hard, stony, or clay-heavy soils
howToUse: Choose rod diameter based on desired sample depth
Rotate the rod while applying downward pressure; taper does the heavy lifting
Use toggle handles (Items 1030 or 1031) to extract the rod smoothly
Clean soil residues with the chisel (Item 1051) to maintain groove integrity between samples

technicalSpecs.Weight: Approx. 2.1 kg per meter (40 mm diameter rod)

table.articleName: Boring bar, effective length 1000 mm, total length 1150 mm, groove 18 mm, conical, round
Boring bar, usable length 1000 mm, total length 1150 mm, groove 18 mm, conical, round with markings in 10 cm sections and broken cutting edges
Boring bar, effective length 1200 mm, total length 1350 mm, groove 18 mm, conical, round
Boring bar, effective length 1500 mm, total length 1650 mm, groove 18 mm, conical, round
Toggle for lifting and emptying the drill rod, length 300 mm
Toggle for lifting and emptying the drill rod, length 600 mm

1 set of sampling probes (N-min drill bit set) for three depths. Each drill bit consists of the actual sampling probe, an extension, and the impact head with a cross-bore.
(0-30 cm Ø 40-38 mm, 30-60 cm Ø 35-33 mm, 60-90 cm Ø 30-28 mm). All parts tool steel, developed for hard soils.
Probe, useful length 0-30 cm, outer diameter 40-38 mm, conical
Extension for the 0-30 cm probe
Probe, usable length 30-60 cm, outer diameter 35-33 mm, conical
Extension for the 30-60 cm probe
Probe, usable length 60-90 cm, outer diameter 30-28 mm, conical
Extension for the 60-90 cm probe
Impact head with cross hole
Dial rod with shrink-fitted impact head for taking soil samples up to 2 m deep, Ø 15 mm, 8x8 groove. Used with NL 1000 percussion drill rod (Art. No. 1001).
Chisel for stripping and removing the soil sample, 18 mm, round
Chisel for stripping and removing the soil sample, 15 mm, round
Chisel for stripping and removing the soil sample, 14 mm, square
Chisel for stripping and removing the soil sample, 8 mm, square
Chisel for stripping and removing the soil sample, 12 mm, square
Impact-lifting combination for impact drill rods and 2 m long dipsticks (square hammer, pull hook, and pull ring). Hammer impact surface 135 x 135 mm, 200 mm long, weight approx. 4300 g.
Square hammer complete
drawing ring
Pulling hook
Stainless steel soil probe for locating soil compaction and soil layers. Effective length 1000 mm with markings at 100 mm intervals, 8 mm diameter.

testimonials.quote: We've used these rods for 15 years—still the most reliable tool for N-min work. The taper makes all the difference in clay.
testimonials.author: Dr. Lena Bauer

drillrods.name: Drill Rods Ø22 mm for Normal Soil Profiles
specs: Drilling groove Ø 22 mm, total length 1100 mm, usable length 1000 mm
12 mm groove for holding soil samples
Extensions 1000 mm long with 5/8” internal thread on both sides
drillrods.description: Robust drill rods (Ø 22 mm) designed to open normal soil profiles up to 10 m depth with modular, interchangeable parts for flexible length adjustment.
drillrods.herodescription: Meet the modular drill rods with a neat 22 mm drilling groove, built to tackle soil profiles up to a serious 10 meters deep. Thanks to connecting bolts, you can adjust the length faster than you can say 'Bob's your uncle,' and the striking heads handle everything—from the casual tap to the full machine punch.
drillrods.detailedDescription:
Each section is a solid 1000 mm of engineering precision, sporting a 12 mm groove to keep your soil samples firmly in place while you pull them up from the depths. Extensions screw on easily with 5/8” internal threads—because sometimes, more is just more.

Tighten with special SW 18 wrenches—no slipping, no drama. For the grand finale, pick your weapon: a jack lifter with clamping chain or a one-arm puller with a ball clamp, both designed to get the job done safely and without breaking a sweat.
features: Ø 22 mm drilling groove with 12 mm soil sample groove
Modular design with interchangeable parts bolted together
1000 mm usable length per rod section
Special striking heads for hand hammer or machine use
Special wrenches for secure tightening
Lifting devices for easy rod extraction
applications: Opening normal soil profiles up to 10 meters depth
Manual soil sampling in non-rocky soils
Flexible rod length adjustment for variable depth sampling
howToUse: Assemble rods and extensions to required length using connecting bolts
Use appropriate striking head with hand hammer or machine
Tighten rod sections using special wrenches (SW 18)
Extract rods using jack lifting device with clamp chain or one-arm puller with ball clamp

technicalSpecs.Lifting devices: Jack with lifting bar and clamping chain, one-arm puller with integrated ball clamp

hammers.name: Hammer Selection & Accessories
hammers.nickname: The Right Hammer for Every Job
specs: Variety of hammers with impact surfaces from 80 mm Ø to 150 mm square
Soft-face and nylon hammers with malleable cast iron housing
Handles made of wood or hickory with various lengths
hammers.description: Every hammer's a hero in its own right. Pick the one that fits your soil-sampling saga and don't forget the trusty sidekicks—our accessories.
hammers.herodescription: From the solid, no-nonsense square hammer to the gentle touch of a nylon soft-face, we've got the tools to make your soil sampling less grunt, more finesse. Choose wisely—precision, power, and comfort await.
hammers.detailedDescription:
Need to channel your inner Thor with a hefty 4.3 kg square hammer boasting a 135x135 mm impact surface? Or perhaps a featherlight nylon soft-face hammer to coax the soil with subtlety? Either way, your wish is our command.

Fancy a bit of DIY flair? Swap heads like a chef changes knives, pick from wooden or hickory handles of all lengths, and top it off with inserts as tough or gentle as you like. It's like a bespoke suit—only for your hammer.
features: Square hammer with 135x135 mm impact surface, 4.3 kg
Round plastic hammer with 150 mm Ø impact surface
Soft-face hammers with malleable cast iron housing
Nylon soft-face hammer variants
Interchangeable hammer heads and inserts
Wooden and hickory handles of varying lengths
applications: Manual soil sampling requiring precise impact force
Protection of delicate soil structures with soft-face hammers
Versatile hammer selection for different sampling equipment
howToUse: Select hammer type according to soil hardness and sampling method
Use appropriate handle length for leverage and comfort
Replace hammer heads or inserts as needed for optimal performance
Maintain wooden handles and tighten screws regularly
